/cwe/weakness/460.Name: Improper Cleanup on Thrown Exception
/cwe/weakness/460.Description: The product does not clean up its state or incorrectly cleans up its state when an exception is thrown, leading to unexpected state or control flow.
/cwe/weakness/460.ExtendedDescription: Often, when functions or loops become complicated, some level of resource cleanup is needed throughout execution. Exceptions can disturb the flow of the code and prevent the necessary cleanup from happening.

ModesOfIntroduction.Note: REALIZATION: This weakness is caused during implementation of an architectural security tactic.

CommonConsequences.Note: The code could be left in a bad state.

Entries.IntroText: The following example demonstrates the weakness.

Entries.BodyText: In this case, a thread might be left locked accidentally.

PotentialMitigations.Description: If one breaks from a loop or function by throwing an exception, make sure that cleanup happens or that you should exit the program. Use throwing exceptions sparsely.
